properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Project D:
The project aims to restore the capacity of the generation units, increase the availability of clean electricity, and strengthen the safety of the dams of the APHC. 

The scope of rehabilitation includes rehabilitation of electrical and mechanical components of generating units and auxiliaries, to address obsolescence and upgrading issues of operational performance and conditions, and increasing the capacity and energy production of the plants through equipment efficiency and increased water availability.


• Rehabilitation of Agus-Pulangi Hydroelectric power plant 
 • Advance stage of Feasibility Study
 • Estimated project cost is Php 20 Billion with the NPC as the implementing agency.


Phase 1: rehabilitation of Agus IV, V, VI, and VII plants; will generate 417.1 MW (Php10.19 B)

Phase 2: rehabilitation of Agus I, II, and Pulangi IV plants; will produce 515 MW (Php6.52 billion)
properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Location:
Marawi City & Saguiaran, Lanao del Sur
Iligan City, Lanao del Norte &
Maramag, Bukidnon
properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Province:
Lanao del Norte
Lanao del Sur
Bukidnon
properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Update:
March 2025
Processing the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)'s recommendations in order to proceed
with the pre-procurement conference for the Transaction Advisory Services for the AgusPulangi Rehabilitation and Asset Management Plan.

Feb 2025
> Per PSALM, the project will be packaged for PPP scheme
properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Update So:
NEDA Board IFPs Updates for Q1 2023

Neda Memo (April 27, 2021)
properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Updates f:
Per NPC source, the project is still in PSALM awaiting for the PPP process which involves the rehabilitation, maintenance and operation of the APHC by the private proponent. 

-Wil Mabale
Feb. 26, 2024
properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Historica:
March 2024
> During the February 13. 2024 ICC-TB meeting, it was agreed that the project will be delisted from the ICC Project Appraisal Monitor (IPAM) given the proposed change in the mode of financing. 
> In a letter of DOF-IFG to WB dated 16 May 2023, the Department informed that the PSALM Corporation has decided to pursue the project through PPP modality instead of ODA financing.

Nov 2023:
> In a letter of DOF to WB dated May 16, 2023, the
Department informed that the PSALM Corporation
has decided to pursue the project through PPP
modality instead of ODA financing.

Aug 2023
> For Government Approval
> For submission by IA of additional ICC requisite documents and clarifications.
> NPC is currently preparing the technical/financial requirements as required by te ICC, for review/processing of its project

March 31, 2023

For submission by IA of additional ICC requisite documents and clarifications.

NPC is currently preparing the technical/financial requirements as required by the ICC, for review/processing of its project proposal.

July 2022:
 Department of Finance (DOF) said it has been finetuning a proposed arrangement with the Power Sector Assets and Liabilities Management Corporation (PSALM) and National Power Corp. (NPC) on the funding rollout and implementation of the estimated PHP16.71-billion rehabilitation projects for the decades-old facilities in the complex.
 
 Awaiting approval from the National Economic and Development Authority and still being evaluated by the DoF, phase one is estimated to cost P10.19 billion.
 
 The second phase involves the Agus I, II, and Pulangi IV plants, which will produce 515 MW after the works are complete. It is estimated to cost P6.52 billion, according to a World Bank study.

============

April 2021:
 Ongoing implementation of Environmental and Social Impact Assessment (ESIA)
 Phase I
 
 Through a letter dated 11 January 2021, NEDA endorsed to DOF the proposed WB Grant on the Preparation of Feasibility Study and Procurement of Owner’s Engineer of the Agus-Pulangi Hydroelectric Power Plants Rehabilitation Project
properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Remarks: RDC X priority and endorsed for 2022

properties.MIP Project Mapping 20260304v2_Project D:
The project aims to restore the capacity of the generation units, increase the availability of clean electricity, and strengthen the safety of the dams of the APHC. 

The scope of rehabilitation includes rehabilitation of electrical and mechanical components of generating units and auxiliaries, to address obsolescence and upgrading issues of operational performance and conditions, and increasing the capacity and energy production of the plants through equipment efficiency and increased water availability.


• Rehabilitation of Agus-Pulangi Hydroelectric power plant 
 • Advance stage of Feasibility Study
 • Estimated project cost is Php 20 Billion with the NPC as the implementing agency.


Phase 1: rehabilitation of Agus IV, V, VI, and VII plants; will generate 417.1 MW (Php10.19 B)

Phase 2: rehabilitation of Agus I, II, and Pulangi IV plants; will produce 515 MW (Php6.52 billion)

March 2024
> During the February 13. 2024 ICC-TB meeting, it was agreed that the project will be delisted from the ICC Project Appraisal Monitor (IPAM) given the proposed change in the mode of financing. 
> In a letter of DOF-IFG to WB dated 16 May 2023, the Department informed that the PSALM Corporation has decided to pursue the project through PPP modality instead of ODA financing.

Department informed that the PSALM Corporation
has decided to pursue the project through PPP
modality instead of ODA financing.

Aug 2023
> For Government Approval
> For submission by IA of additional ICC requisite documents and clarifications.
> NPC is currently preparing the technical/financial requirements as required by te ICC, for review/processing of its project

March 31, 2023

For submission by IA of additional ICC requisite documents and clarifications.

NPC is currently preparing the technical/financial requirements as required by the ICC, for review/processing of its project proposal.
